creator: _jkrow
caption:
Once in a lifetime glamping? Yes please.

If you’ve looked into the best places to stay in Torres del Paine National Park, then you’ve probably come across EcoCamp Patagonia, the most unique and sustainable lodging option in Chilean Patagonia 🇨🇱 

At about $1000 for a 2 night stay (for the cheapest room), I was definitely hesitant to stay here. We don’t usually splurge that much on one stay when we travel, but since we were traveling all the way to Patagonia, we figured we might as well go all in…so we grabbed the one night that was still available 🤷🏼‍♀️

With its epic location to the Base Torres hike, eco friendly design, and immersive nature experience (no cell service or WiFi here!), this place seemed like a stay worth experiencing at least once in a lifetime.

But is it really worth the splurge? Lmk what you think 👀

👇 Say “ECOCAMP” and I’ll send you the 🔗 to my full blog post with all the details, a full recap of our stay + all of the best hotels in Torres del Paine National Park 

#patagonia #travelguide #glamping

transcription.text: This is the ultimate luxury glamping experience in one of the most beautiful places in the world. Eco Camp Patagonia is one of the world's most sustainable hotels and it's located in the middle of Chile's Torres del Paine National Park, one of the most remote national parks on the planet. Eco Camp Patagonia has a total of 33 domes of various sizes and amenities. These domes are inspired by ancient shelters used by the region's indigenous people and are designed to have the smallest environmental impact possible. The entire camp is carbon neutral and runs on solar wind and hydropower. All of the domes and walkways are built to be low impact and easily removable and it almost feels like the whole thing could be packed up and moved in a moment's notice. You're completely off grid here but honestly you'd never know it because there's heat, hot water, a full bar with a lounge area, and chef prepared meals which are obviously served with local Chilean wine. Most people stay here because it's so close to the Mirador Las Torres hike. You can actually start the hike directly from camp which is ideal if this hike is on your radar. So if you're looking for a way to immerse yourself in the wilderness of Patagonia without totally roughing it then a stay at Eco Camp might be worth the splurge.
